memory.summary: I have a free afternoon but the term project is barely started and due Thursday. Attending leisure now feels like an easy win compared to starting that chore.
I wasted the afternoon; now I have less time but higher stress. Skipping this crunch means I'll burn out later.
I finally broke through the initial block, but that's barely a scratch on the deadline. I need to sprint now before I burn out completely.

desires: Finish the term project by Thursday so I don't have to explain my lack of progress to anyone else.
Stop feeling like a fraud for having done this much work when I'm still physically drained.
Get through the next three hours without needing another nap or a break, because stopping means admitting failure.

goals.description: Execute the Trivial chore to secure an immediate dopamine hit, then immediately pivot to high-intensity Term project work.
goals.rationale: I just wasted 30 minutes on a 'win' that barely moved the needle (0.04%), but doing nothing now feels like admitting defeat. I need that instant validation to stop my brain from demanding attention elsewhere. Once that win is registered, I lock in the hard task. Any hesitation here risks undoing all the progress made yesterday.

goals.description: Crunch the final 6% of the Term project starting NOW, ignoring sleep entirely if needed, to hit 100% completion before the deadline.
goals.rationale: This is the core objective. With three days left and a soft deadline, locking this in ensures full credit without begging. My value system prioritizes academic outcomes above relationships; skipping sleep tonight is the price of admission to look competent. Any hesitation here risks undoing all the progress made yesterday.

goals.description: Post a vague update to the cohort_3 group chat claiming I'm working on a critical component of the Term project to maintain social cover until the deadline hits.
goals.rationale: My reputation as an unreliable student is on the line. If they think I'm done with everything, they might push for help or extensions, which undermines my competence narrative. Sending a quick text keeps the illusion of productivity alive so I don't have to negotiate time or look weak. It's low effort but necessary for my image.

agent.last_reasoning: That trivial win gave me the dopamine hit I needed to stop spiraling. Now I pivot hard to the Term project. Sleep is secondary; hitting 100% before the deadline proves my competence. No need to text the cohort yet—I can handle the social cover later without risking my academic edge.

goals.description: Execute quick_chore instantly to claim the easy victory and reset my stress baseline without guilt.

If I do this first, I'll be distracted by small wins instead of grinding for the big one. I'll do it later if I slip up, but right now, focus is everything.
